description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a tiled, pitched roof supported by wooden beams. The well's stone wall is constructed from stacked, gray masonry blocks, forming a sturdy and protective barrier around the open shaft. The roof is covered with red, rectangular tiles, showing some weathering and moss, and is supported by a wooden frame with visible cross-bracing. A metal crank handle is attached to one side, suggesting a manual water-drawing mechanism, though no bucket or rope is visible. The well sits on a circular, paved stone platform, which is slightly weathered and mossy at the edges. The overall design is rustic and functional, suitable for placement in parks, courtyards, or village squares.
description: A circular stone water well with a red-tiled pitched roof, wooden supports, and a metal crank handle, set on a round stone platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, red-tiled roof, wooden supports, and a metal crank handle on the right side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the wooden cross-bracing of the roof and the open back of the well, with no crank handle visible.
description_view_2: Front view similar to view 0, with the crank handle now on the left side and the stone well and roof clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear angled view showing the wooden roof supports and the open well, with the stone platform and mossy details visible.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A metal crank handle is present on the side, but no pulley or bucket is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well; pitched roof supported by wooden beams acts as a canopy.
